weather_report:
Osborne Park wakes under a lucid blue bowl of sky, the sun already clean and uncomplicated by late morning. The air looks sharp, shadows are defined, and the light has that winter brilliance that flatters every surface. Expect the afternoon to hold steady with the same untroubled clarity; plenty of sunshine and strong midday glare. Even in June, the UV will be modest-to-moderate, enough to warrant sunglasses and a touch of sunscreen if you linger outside.

It reads as a pleasant day: cool in the shade, gently warm where the sun finds you. Dress in light layers, a breathable long sleeve or a soft jacket you can shrug off, plus a hat if you’re out around lunch. Good hours for ordinary comforts—walk to pick up a sandwich, let the house breathe with the windows ajar, hang a load of washing, water the herbs, or take a short spin on the bike path. The kind of clear day that rewards unhurried errands and a quiet seat in the sun.
